Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First, the temporal profile is a dictionary where each key is a tuple of two activities that can follow each other in a trace not just directly consecutive but eventually in some order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the sequence of activities in the process, the typical order would be something like SS OP RC QI CA PT PK WS DT AS

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Therefore, for any two activities where the first is earlier in this sequence than the second, the pair X Y is possible

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: But the problem says a representative subset of these activity pairs so we do not need to include all possible pairs just a representative subset that shows complexity by considering pairs separated by multiple steps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets think about realistic times for each step

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: First Supplier Selection to Order Placement Selecting a supplier might take some time maybe a few days to weeks depending on negotiations Lets say average 3 days with a standard deviation of 1 day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Placement to Receive Components This is the supplier lead time For high tech components this could be weeks to months Lets say average 14 days std dev 3 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Receiving components and then inspecting them Inspection might take a few hours to a day Average 12 hours std dev 6 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After passing inspection components go to assembly Maybe a day or two for scheduling assembly Average 1 day std dev 12 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Assembling the sub unit and then testing Assembly might take a few hours testing could take a day Lets say average 2 days std dev 1 day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Testing passes then packaging Packaging might be quick but maybe some delay Average 4 hours std dev 2 hours

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Packaged product stored in warehouse Storage time could vary maybe a few days to weeks Average 5 days std dev 2 days

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Stored until shipping Shipping might be scheduled periodically so average 3 days std dev 1 day

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Distribution to retailers customers then after sales support After sales could start after some time maybe average 1 month std dev 1 week

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For non consecutive pairs the average time would be the sum of the averages of the intermediate steps

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The standard deviation is not simply the sum because they might be independent but for estimation purposes we can approximate the standard deviation as the square root of the sum of squares of the standard deviations assuming independence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Supplier selection to receiving components selecting supplier 3 days then order placement 14 days lead time so 17 days total standard deviation 5 days seems reasonable

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Inspection to packaging total about 3 point 17 days standard deviation 1 day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Assembly to warehouse storage total about 7 point 17 days standard deviation about 2 point 2 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Packaging to after sales 38 days standard deviation about 7 point 3 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The whole process about 58 point 67 days standard deviation about 8 days

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Compiling a list of 12 to 15 pairs that show different separations
